title: [CI:DOCS] install.systemd: set podman's install path
body:
Set Podman's install path in the systemd service file.

Fixes: #10787
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Not sure what to do with the `git status` checks _after_ `make install.systemd`

> Not sure what to do with the `git status` checks _after_ `make install.systemd`

Maybe, it should run `sed` on the installed files instead?

> Should we instead of using the absolute path just use podman and let $PATH handle it. This should work for everyone unless they do not have podman in $PATH which should be unlikely.

Ya, this seems the right thing to do.

```
The following environment variables are propagated by the service manager or generated internally for 
each invoked process:

$PATH
Colon-separated list of directories to use when launching executables. systemd uses a fixed value of 
"/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin" in the system manager. When compiled for systems with 
"unmerged /usr/" (/bin is not a symlink to /usr/bin), ":/sbin:/bin" is appended. In case of the user manager, 
a different path may be configured by the distribution. It is recommended to not rely on the order of entries, 
and have only one program with a given name in $PATH.
```

So to me that says, just don't use a path :smile:

I am going to close the PR and the issue as won't fix.  IMHO, it's common to ship hard-coded (and absolute) paths in .service files and I think we're getting more problems than we'd fix by changing the current state.

The problem is, that the hard-codes paths are wrong.

This should work out of the box on a Linux from scratch system.  Please add '/local' in ExecStart=/usr/bin and that is all.
